description_long: This fence is constructed from thick, roughly hewn wooden logs, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The structure consists of two vertical posts and two horizontal rails, with an additional diagonal brace for reinforcement. The wood displays a weathered texture, with visible grain and natural imperfections, suggesting outdoor use and exposure to the elements. The ends of the posts are cut at an angle, and there are no decorative elements or caps on the posts. The overall design is utilitarian, likely intended to serve as a simple barrier or boundary in a park, rural, or urban green space. The coloration is a dark, natural brown, consistent with untreated or minimally treated timber.
description: A rustic wooden fence made of thick, weathered logs with two horizontal rails and a diagonal brace, featuring a rough, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the vertical posts and the angled cut at the top.
description_view_1: Top-down view revealing the arrangement of the horizontal rails and the diagonal brace between the posts.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the depth and solid construction of the fence.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the full face of the fence, including both posts, rails, and the diagonal brace.
